techniques.evidence_quote: Initial Access T1078 ... Exposed IAM Access keys in Public S3

SES commands issued to verify new domain, DKIM and email address
New IAM Resource created, assigned additional permissions

assigned additional permissions and roles for SES & SQS (AttachUserPolicy)

attack_chain.description: Attacker discovered exposed IAM access keys in a public S3 bucket and used them for initial access via AWS CLI.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Earliest evidence of compromise March 29th, 2024. Exposed IAM Access keys in Public S3

evidence_chunks.quote: Earliest evidence of compromise March 29th, 2024. Exposed IAM Access keys in Public S3
Earliest evidence of compromise March 29th, 2024. Exposed IAM Access keys in Public S3

attack_chain.description: Used AWS CLI to issue StartInstance and RequestServiceQuotaIncrease calls, probing account capabilities.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Request via AWS CLI to 'Start Instance' & 'RequestServiceQuotaIncrease'

attack_chain.description: Created new IAM users/resources via CLI to establish persistence.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Via CLI New IAM Resource created, assigned additional permissions

evidence_chunks.quote: Via CLI New IAM Resource created, assigned additional permissions
Via CLI New IAM Resource created, assigned additional permissions

attack_chain.description: Attached policies/roles to newly created IAM users granting SES and SQS permissions (AttachUserPolicy).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Two new IAM Resource created & assigned additional permissions and roles for SES & SQS (AttachUserPolicy)

evidence_chunks.quote: Two new IAM Resource created & assigned additional permissions and roles for SES & SQS (AttachUserPolicy)
Two new IAM Resource created & assigned additional permissions and roles for SES & SQS (AttachUserPolicy)

attack_chain.description: Deployed new microservice and requested increases in SES sending quota, sending rate, and SNS quota.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: New microservice deployed. Increase Sending Quote initiated & Sending Rate, and SNS quota

evidence_chunks.quote: New microservice deployed. Increase Sending Quote initiated & Sending Rate, and SNS quota
New microservice deployed. Increase Sending Quote initiated & Sending Rate, and SNS quota

attack_chain.description: Issued SES commands to verify a new domain, DKIM, and email address to prepare for mass mailing.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: SES commands issued to verify new domain, DKIM and email address

evidence_chunks.quote: SES commands issued to verify new domain, DKIM and email address
SES commands issued to verify new domain, DKIM and email address

attack_chain.description: Deleted profile elements, policies and access keys for a previously created user from an external IP to hide activity.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Delete profile elements, policies and access keys for a previously created user, executed from external IP not owned by customer

evidence_chunks.quote: Delete profile elements, policies and access keys for a previously created user, executed from external IP not owned by customer
Delete profile elements, policies and access keys for a previously created user, executed from external IP not owned by customer

attack_chain.description: Used hijacked SES resources to send abnormally large volume of emails in a mass phishing campaign; AWS contacted customer about SES bounce rate.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Sending an abnormally large amount of emails in mass phishing campaign. Customer contacted by AWS re SES email bounce rate issue

evidence_chunks.quote: Sending an abnormally large amount of emails in mass phishing campaign. Customer contacted by AWS re SES email bounce rate issue
Sending an abnormally large amount of emails in mass phishing campaign. Customer contacted by AWS re SES email bounce rate issue

initial_access_vector: Exposed IAM access keys found in a public S3 bucket, used via AWS CLI from an external IP.
persistence_mechanism: Creation of new IAM users with attached policies granting SES/SQS permissions (AttachUserPolicy).
privilege_escalation: Account manipulation - attached additional IAM policies/roles to newly created IAM users.
